Alongside technical growth, you’ll develop soft skills and build connections with other interns, locally in your office, regionally across EMEA, and globally, through programs designed just for interns. As a Software Engineer Intern on the Developer Enablement team, you’ll contribute directly to building and scaling applications that utilize environmental data, delivered through a unified standardized interface. You’ll receive mentorship, guidance, and support, while also having the autonomy and responsibility to make a real impact. **Responsibilities** * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to understand the full software development lifecycle * Contribute to real commercial development projects, guided by experienced Autodesk developers * Gain hands\-on experience with TypeScript, AWS, automated testing frameworks, DevOps, and more * Apply Agile and Scrum practices, building your project management skills * Use source control and Continuous Integration platforms such as GitHub and Jenkins * Develop robust APIs to enable sustainability analysis for Autodesk customers * Share your progress and achievements in team meetings, gaining feedback and recognition **Minimum Qualifications** * Currently pursuing a degree in a STEM field (e.g., Computer Science, Engineering) * Expected graduation date: please include your month and year of graduation in your application * Experience with API development (REST and GraphQL), through coursework, personal projects, or previous internships * Programming experience (TypeScript preferred) * Enthusiasm for learning new technologies and a proactive approach to problem\-solving **The Ideal Candidate** * Familiarity with automated testing principles to help write reliable, maintainable code * Familiarity with AWS or other cloud platforms, giving you a head start in cloud\-based development * Additional experience with other programming languages or frameworks is a plus * Strong communication skills and ability to work in a team environment * Previous internship experience in a related field is advantageous Learn More **About Autodesk** Welcome to Autodesk! Amazing things are created every day with our software – from the greenest buildings and cleanest cars to the smartest factories and biggest hit movies. We help innovators turn their ideas into reality, transforming not only how things are made, but what can be made. We take great pride in our culture here at Autodesk – it’s at the core of everything we do. Our culture guides the way we work and treat each other, informs how we connect with customers and partners, and defines how we show up in the world. When you’re an Autodesker, you can do meaningful work that helps build a better world designed and made for all. Ready to shape the world and your future? Join us! **Salary transparency** Salary is one part of Autodesk’s competitive compensation package. Offers are based on the candidate’s experience, educational level, and geographic location. **Diversity \& Belonging** We take pride in cultivating a culture of belonging where everyone can thrive. title: Internal Auditor
description: **Overview** PM Group’s Internal Audit is a group\-wide function responsible for delivering the annual internal audit plan, promoting effective internal controls and risk management. Internal Audit’s key role is to provide independent assurance to the Audit Committee and Senior Management that the organisations risk management, governance and internal control processes are operating effectively. Due to the continued expansion of the Group, an opportunity has arisen within the Internal Audit function. The successful candidate will work closely with the Head of Internal Audit to assist in the delivery of the agreed audit schedule included in the annual audit plan. The role will involve some travel globally ( *circa 10 to 15%)* and offers a hybrid working arrangement. **Why Internal Audit?** While working within Internal Audit you will have an opportunity to see the inner workings of the business. You can increase your knowledge of how the overall business operates, work cross functionally, develop internal relationships and enhance your reporting and presenting skills. The work is varied and touches on all aspects of the business. Your recommendations can bring real improvement and value to the business. **Who are we?** PM Group is a leading international Engineering, Architecture, Project and Construction Management firm. With a network of offices in Europe, Asia and the US and a multi\-discipline team of over 3, people, we deliver complex, capital projects in the Pharmaceutical, Med Tech, Food, Mission Critical and Advanced Manufacturing Sectors. **Responsibilities** * Planning, executing and completing reviews of financial and operational processes throughout PM Group in line with the Internal Audit Plan. * Producing detailed working papers and drafting clear, concise reports and obtaining management responses prior to their finalisation. * Offering insights, expertise, and advice and constructively challenging current controls. * Ensuring assignments are delivered in defined time scales. * Working with the business to ensure that issues raised are addressed and recommendations implemented in a timely manner. * Performing follow\-up audits to ensure appropriate action plans raised in both internal and external audit reports have been implemented and are operating effectively. * Assisting in the preparation of Audit Committee and Senior Management communications. * Working closely with Head of Internal Audit and other Heads of business units, including Finance, Risk and Legal to improve corporate governance, risk management and internal controls. * Building strong professional relationships with the leadership in the business. * Encouraging the practice of strong internal controls across the group. * Liaising with external auditors as required. **Qualifications** * Strong interpersonal and communication skills that will enable you to persuade others and build effective relationships across cultures. * Excellent attention to detail. * Strong process, technical and analytical skills, combined with strong commercial acumen. * Member of a recognised Accounting Body and at least 2 years PQE. * Ability to challenge management constructively on established practices and policies where necessary. * Ability to work on own initiative while also being a good team player. * Comfortable interacting with Middle and Senior Management across the organisation. * Act in a professional and ethical manner at all times and observing the confidential nature of Internal Audit’s work. * Ability to establish and retain effective working relationships with staff and management. * Ability to work effectively with tight timelines, high quality standards and demanding, potentially overlapping priorities. * Working knowledge of Microsoft Office programs, specifically, Excel, Word, Outlook and PowerPoint. * Familiarity with Unit 4 Business World (formerly Agresso) an advantage. * Experience using Power BI/Analytical tools would also be an advantage. **Why PM Group?** As an employee\-owned company, we are inclusive, committed and driven. Corporate Responsibility and Sustainability are the heart of our new 2025 business strategy. Click here to read more in our **Corporate Responsibility and Heath, Safety \& Well\-being Report.** Inclusion and Diversity are core to our culture and values. Wherever we work, we commit to a culture of mutual respect and belonging by building a truly inclusive workplace rich in diverse people, talents and ideas. Valuing the contributions of all our people and respecting individual differences will sustain our growth into the future. PM Group is committed to ensuring our hiring process is fair and accessible to all and will provide candidates with disabilities with reasonable accommodations required to participate in the recruitment process. If you require any assistance in this regard, please let us know.

I'm tasked with looking for an experienced Java Developer to join their high\-performing engineering team where ownership, collaboration, and code quality are central to how they work. **The Role** You'll contribute across the full dev cycle. So, that means designing, building, and maintaining scalable Java app, participating in code reviews, mentoring junior developers, and feeding into sprint planning and technical estimation. Continuous improvement across the team is big for them! **Essential Requirements** * Degree in Computer Science or related field * 3\+ years of Java development experience * Strong knowledge of Java frameworks (Spring, Hibernate, Java EE) * Solid understanding of OOP, design patterns, and architectural principles * Experience with relational databases (MySQL, Oracle, MS SQL Server) and NoSQL (MongoDB, DynamoDB, Cosmos DB) * Git version control proficiency * Agile experience (Scrum or Kanban) * Strong problem\-solving, debugging, and communication skills **Advantageous** * Microservices and containerisation experience (Docker, Kubernetes) * Familiarity with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript * CI/CD and DevOps exposure **What We Offer** A collaborative, technically ambitious environment where engineers have real ownership over their work. If you're a motivated developer who takes pride in delivering quality software and wants to grow within a forward\-thinking team, we'd love to hear from you. *At this time we are only open to considering EU citizens, those with Stamp 1G Spousal or Stamp 4 Visas and who are current permanently based in Ireland, thank you.*

title: Junior Butcher - F.X. Buckley Victualler & Grocer, Dublin 2
description: **Responsible To: Butcher Manager / Operations Manager** We are looking for a junior butcher to work alongside existing team carrying out all aspects of butchery/ retail shop ensuring our customers receive the best possible service in a timely, pleasant manor. This is a front of house public facing / customer service role, in a high\-end shop, and a professional clean\-cut appearance is required. You must be compliant with Food safety/Health \& Safety regulations always adhering to best practise. Ideally you will have previous butchery and retail experience and strong communications skills. This is a Full\-time, permanent position, 5 days per week. Applicants must be able to work Saturdays with flexibility provided during the week. The You are required to participate in the training provided by the Company. Pay is negotiable but dependant on ability and experience. You must be able to provide a work reference/s. Applicants must have permission to legally work in Ireland.

title: Junior Buyer
description: **Position:** Junior Buyer **Division:** Procurement **Reports To:** Procurement Manager **Date Prepared:** April 2025 **Role Description:** Responsible for the day\-to\-day of the Junior Buyer of the Procurement Department by supporting the wider team in all procurement functions along with sole responsibility for the ordering \& overall management of the workwear/PPE orders \& distribution. **Key Accountabilities:** * Generating, processing \& amending purchase orders. * Draft purchase orders and issue to the relevant stakeholders. * Monitor stock levels of office supplies to include but not limited to stationery and consumables. * Manage the procurement storeroom ensuring that it is kept in a presentable manner and all stock is recorded accordingly. * First point of contact for collection of materials/assets by site personnel. * Assist the procurement team in asset tagging all tools \& equipment to include asset transfers. * Support the procurement \& fleet co\-ordinator as and when required. * Support the wider team ensuring that all suppliers have the appropriate accreditations and insurances cover. * Liaise with all facilities \& maintenance teams to include key account managers as required. * Manage internal requisitions and request multiple quotes to ensure the best price. * Monitor supplier performance and internal stakeholders and escalate issues and concerns as they arise. * Forecast price and market trends while identifying key changes that may impact on purchasing and give feedback to the Procurement Manager for consideration \& further analysis. * Identify cost reduction opportunities to achieve the departments financial objectives. * Keep up to date on market trends and innovations, regulations and new technology which may have an impact on how the business will function in the future. * Identify potential areas of improvement based on observations and make recommendations to the Procurement Manager. * Responsible for the day\-to\-day operations of the procurement department to include the shared inbox management inclusive of the shared management of the invoice register. * Understand the range and depth of the services provided by Sensori FM with the eagerness to further develop your knowledge of the business and the services we provide. * Support the Company in promoting a culture of excellent customer service and establish exceptional working relationships with all key stakeholders. * Act as a brand ambassador and lead the Company through its ongoing expansion. * Any other duties as deemed necessary for the efficiency of the business **Critical Competencies:** * Demonstrate the ability to manage procurement \& support services provided to Amazon Account. * Demonstrate the ability to manage Workwear \& PPE stock. * Ability to work in a proactive and reactive manner. * Ability to multitask \& prioritise workload. * Ability to work under pressure. * Demonstrate tangible business improvements. * Compliance with Company policies \& procedures **Qualifications, Experience and Knowledge:** * Preferably has an Irish Institute of Purchasing \& Materials Management (IIPMM) degree or equivalent qualification. * 1\+ years' experience in an administration role. * Proficient IT skills and working knowledge /use of Microsoft Office (Word, Excel etc). * Solid commercial \& financial awareness. * Excellent analytical \& planning ability with exceptional numeracy skills. * Strong negotiation skills. * Positive \& professional manner. * Conscientious with a strong attention to detail. * Organised with the ability to meet strict deadlines.

title: Junior Electrical Engineer
description: RGN Electrical \& Communications Ltd, are looking at expanding our office support team and looking for a motivated Junior Electrical Engineer/Project Co\-ordinator. We are based in Naas Co. Kildare and the role will be based primarily in our offices, with weekly site visits. The successful candidate will be responsible for: * Electrical Design review – identifying design deviations, contradictions, or scope gaps * Submitting RFIs and Technical Queries to clients and Consultants * Prepare, issue, and manage Electrical Technical submittals * Completing project close out documentation and as con drawings – CAD and PDF * Occasional site visits and surveys to monitor installations and quality reports * Ensure systems are compliant to specifications and drawings * To assist the commercial team on tendering new and upcoming Projects * General change orders and variations on current projects. * General Project Administration The ideal candidate will have the following experiences and qualities. * Motivated Self starter * Engineering Degree, Higher certificate or equivilant trade * Exceptional attention to detail * Team Player * Experienced in meeting tight deadlines and a fast paced working environment * Effective communicator and problem solver * Highly proficient on Microsoft office, CAD * Drivers license and Car essential * Fluent English essential This is a great opportunity for someone who wants to progress in this industry with a growing reputable company. Based in the Naas office. This role offers huge potential for the right person to grow into a more senior role with an ever\-expanding company. Job Type: Full\-time

title: Junior Graphic Designer
description: We have an exciting opportunity for a Graphic Designer to join our expanding design studio Stray Dog, which is part of the Doggett Group, a leading Print Management Company in Harold’s Cross, Dublin 12\. This role is varied, from design layout to pre\-press, so an understanding of digital and pre\-press file setup is essential. Experience: * Graphic design for print: 1 year (required) Work Location: In person

title: Junior HR Business Partner
description: **Company:** ------------ Mercer **Description:** ---------------- **We are seeking a talented individual to join our Human Resource team at Marsh in Ireland.This is a hybrid role that has a requirement of working at least three days a week in the office.** Reporting to the Lead HR Manager, the successful candidate will be responsible for the provision of comprehensive Human Resource services in a successful and growing organization. As a **Junior HR Business Partner,** you will play an important role within the People Function and will support our businesses across Ireland. In this role, you will coach, support and advise People Managers based in Ireland, aiming to develop and enhance the quality of people management throughout the colleague lifecycle; this role can also include project work. You will have the opportunity to advise and support on people matters in a business where the People function is considered an essential partner. The Junior HR Business Partner will also be key to the supporting the deployment and adoption of new processes or initiatives which are aimed at People Managers. Youwill need to understand and reflect the local market perspective whilst respecting the value of enterprise\-wide alignment where appropriate. **We will count on you to:** * Support the deployment of enterprise\-wide HR programs and initiatives. * Provide guidance and support to Managers across the full range of activities in the HR portfolio, including and not limited to performance management, skills and competence development and learning, succession planning, absent/leave management, change management, organizational effectiveness, colleague engagement, compensation, and total rewards including the year\-end process. * Assist in employee relations matters such as performance improvement processes, disciplinary actions, grievances, and appeals, working closely with more experienced colleagues and the Employee Relations CoE for guidance on complex cases. * Supporting the rollout and delivery of HR programs and contributing to process improvements. * Cultivate strong relationships with functional teams (Finance, Total Rewards, Talent Acquisition and Benefits) and support the alignment between the people and business strategies. * Support People Managers and colleagues during organizational changes by assisting with coordination of activities and consultations, working alongside senior HR colleagues to ensure smooth transitions. * Consult and partner with the wider HR network and CoEs to determine best practices, continuously improve delivery methods, and especially consider country\-specific nuances to guide local implementation. * Work with the HR Team and Centres of Expertise to understand and support compliance with regulatory and statutory requirements, contributing to reporting and related activities as part of your development. **What you will need to have:** * 2\+ years of relevant work experience in HR alongside a requirement for relevant qualifications. * Able to operate without supervision for straightforward matters and able to judge when guidance is required for more complex situations and seek support from appropriate colleagues/experts * Contribute to the successful implementation of HR projects and initiatives by supporting project activities and collaborating with stakeholders, gaining experience in aligning HR efforts with business objectives. * Experience in supporting managers with employee relations cases, with a knowledge of relevant Irish legislation. * Able to support People Managers in specific groups within the country on all aspects of the employee life cycle in all typical situations. * Able to identify opportunities to make improvements to the service provided by People Services, and demonstrates a willingness and aptitude to embrace changes that will deliver a better service to People Managers * Effective communications and change management skills. * Attention to detail with high personal work standards. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment, with shifting priorities and a complex matrix organization. **What makes you stand out?** * Bachelors’ degree * CIPD qualified or HR related certifications * Previous experience in the financial services sector, or experience in a regulated company * Familiarity with HR system, like Workday or similar systems, * Demonstrated passion for regular self\-investment to maintain up\-to\-date professional knowledge and competence and enthusiasm for learning new skills like Artificial intelligence **Why join our team:** * We’re looking for someone with a great attitude and a collaborative spirit, eager to join an exciting, dynamic team that thrives on flexibility and adaptability to support a wide range of stakeholders and priorities—an excellent opportunity for someone passionate about learning and growing their HR career. * To be part of a company with a strong brand, committed to making a difference in people's lives. * We help you be your best through professional development opportunities, interesting work and supportive leaders. * We foster a vibrant and inclusive culture where you can work with talented colleagues to create new solutions and have impact for colleagues, clients and communities. * Our scale enables us to provide a range of career opportunities, as well as benefits and rewards to enhance your well\-being. * Marsh is a Certified 'Great Place to Work', June 2025 \- June 2026, Best Workplaces and Best Workplaces for Women. Marsh (NYSE: MRSH) is a global leader in risk, reinsurance and capital, people and investments, and management consulting, advising clients in 130 countries. With annual revenue of over $27 billion and more than 95,000 colleagues, Marsh helps build the confidence to thrive through the power of perspective. For more information, visit corporate.marsh.com, or follow us on LinkedIn and X. ### **Marsh is committed to embracing a diverse, inclusive and flexible work environment. We aim to attract and retain the best people and embrace diversity of age, background, disability, ethnic origin, family duties, gender orientation or expression, marital status, nationality, parental status, personal or social status, political affiliation, race, religion and beliefs, sex/gender, sexual orientation or expression, skin color, Traveller community, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We are an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to providing reasonable support to any candidate with a disability/health condition to allow them to fully participate in the recruitment process. We welcome candidates to contact us at reasonableaccommodations@mmc.com to discuss any specific needs.** ### **Marsh is committed to hybrid work, which includes the flexibility of working remotely and the collaboration, connections and professional development benefits of working together in the office. All Marsh colleagues are expected to be in their local office or working onsite with clients at least three days per week. Office\-based teams will identify at least one “anchor day” per week on which their full team will be together in person.**

title: Junior Lighting Design
description: **About the Company** The LED Group, an Irish owned company, has a global presence and a reputation worldwide for first\-class customer service and products. LED Group currently employs over 200 people worldwide. LED Group has ISO9001:2008 Quality Assurance Accreditation and insists upon uncompromising attention to detail and care throughout all processes and practices within the Group. The ROBUS range of products integrates new technologies with an emphasis on high\-performance LEDs, focusing on energy savings and functionality. LED Group’s core values are that of accountability – we always do what we say we will do, ambition, empowerment, customer focus and fast pace. As a result of increased customer demand for lighting designs coupled with strong sales growth, an opportunity has arisen for an in\-house Lighting Designer role within the Lighting Design Department at LED Group in the Dublin office. The right candidate will perform a pivotal role in supporting the Sales Team to win projects by providing top\-class lighting designs and quick turnaround times. This is a junior role, full training and support will be given to the successful applicant. The ideal candidate would be enthusiastic, focused, and energetic with a huge appetite to learn. This role is based in our head office. **Role Overview** Lighting Design Engineer supporting sales and project delivery by creating CAD\-based lighting designs, managing multiple projects, and providing technical and tendering support using tools like Relux or Dialux. **Responsibilities** * Working with the Sales Team primarily, and other key stakeholders in the delivery of lighting design projects. * Review and interpret CAD files to create lighting designs that meet specification requirements. * Deliver lighting designs as requested by the Sales Team within agreed timelines. * Project Management, take ownership of lighting design projects allocated. * Support the Sales team in the tendering process. * Carry out a comparative analysis of ROBUS vs Competitor products through lighting designs and reports. * Assist in technical support functions as business needs demand. **Required Skills** * Basic level of Lighting design experience using Relux or Dialux. * Level 8 Technical Graduate in Engineering or Design. * Experience in working with CAD. * A self\-starter with excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Ability to work well in a team environment as well as being able to work individually. * Results focused, the ability to handle large volumes of data and to run multiple projects concurrently. * Persistent and diligent with a focus on attention to detail. * Assertive and dynamic with a willingness to learn and take on responsibility. **Preferred Skills** * Relevant experience in the lighting industry. * Completion of College modules linked to Lighting Design. * Understanding of building services. * Knowledge of BIM is preferred. * Proven track record of office and industrial lighting design. * Experience in working with CAD files to create suitable lighting designs. * Good working knowledge of lighting\-related standards, schemes, and calculations. **Benefits** * 22 days holidays * Subsidised canteen on site. * Pension scheme available (after 6 months). * Company laptop. * Bike to Work Scheme. * Social Activities. * Personal ADR training.

title: Junior Procurement Engineer
description: **Company Information and Introduction:** Weltec Engineering is a well\-established provider of Mechanical and Electrical engineering services, delivering complex, high\-quality solutions across Europe. We support major clients in the Semiconductor, Pharma/Biopharma, and Advanced Manufacturing sectors and are committed to the highest standards of safety, quality, and engineering excellence. Due to continued growth, we are seeking an experienced and detail\-driven Junior Procurement Engineer to join our project team in Leixlip. The Junior Procurement Engineer will support the procurement function within an M\&E contracting environment, assisting in sourcing materials, managing supplier relationships, and ensuring timely and cost\-effective delivery of mechanical and electrical components for projects. The role will suit a motivated engineering graduate looking to develop a career in procurement within the construction sector. **Key Responsibilities and Duties:** * Assist in sourcing and procuring mechanical and electrical materials, equipment, and services. * Support preparation of enquiries, requests for quotations (RFQs), and tender documentation. * Evaluate supplier quotations and assist in commercial and technical comparisons. * Liaise with project teams (engineering, design, and site teams) to understand procurement requirements. * Track orders and ensure timely delivery in line with project schedules. * Maintain accurate procurement records and documentation. * Support supplier negotiations under the guidance of senior team members. * Monitor supplier performance and resolve delivery or quality issues. * Assist in identifying cost\-saving opportunities and alternative suppliers. * Ensure compliance with company procurement procedures and health \& safety requirements. **Key Skills and Competencies:*** Relevant Level 7 degree in Engineering (e.g., Mechanical, Electrical, Building Services, or similar) * Strong organisational and time management skills * Good communication and interpersonal skills * Attention to detail and accuracy * Ability to work as part of a team in a fast\-paced environment * Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook) * Eagerness to learn and develop within procurement and construction At Weltec Engineering, we believe in recognizing and rewarding the contributions of our team members with a competitive salary that reflects industry standards and individual achievements. We are an equal opportunity employer and encourage candidates from diverse backgrounds to apply.

title: Junior Sales Associate
description: Watch our Careers video on YouTube \- **https://youtu. be/9DzEDYP8MRc** **The Role.** For those interested in a career as a Financial Advisor, IPF is the place to be. We pay our people more, train them better, and invest more in their careers than any other financial institution or company in Ireland. We have a proud history of producing the top financial planners in the industry. You will be working towards becoming a Financial Advisor. As a key member of the team, you will be working with and learning from a team of established financial advisors. Working within our Career Path program, your initial duties \& responsibilities will consist of: * Reviewing/upselling existing customer benefit packages * Working to KPI’s and meeting sales targets * Setting up appointments for the Financial Advisors on your team * For full information, please see our Career Path \- https://www.ipf.ie/careers.html **The Company.** Irish Pensions \& Finance, formed in 1993, has over 30,000 clients and in excess of €1 billion in funds under management. Roughly 80% of our business book comes from the Public Sector, where we administer 152 Union\-sponsored, salary\-deducted AVC schemes. Though most of our 115 employees are based in our offices in Dublin 15, we cover the entire country with more and more of our business being transacted remotely. **The Successful Candidate.** We are looking for individuals who are ready to commit to a career as a Financial Advisor. This is a consultative sales\-focused position. You must have one year of sales experience and have a track record in consistently hitting and exceeding KPIs. The candidate must be comfortable making calls, listening to clients, building solutions, and working to KPI’s \& targets. The approach we take with our clients is very much consultative, and our objective is to have advisors with necessary qualifications, integrity, and commitment to give clients the correct advice. You must be focused, ambitious and have a realistic understanding of the relationship between hard work and success. We can only work with people whose integrity is beyond reproach. **The Package.** * Year 1: €55k OTE (Basic, Bonus, Commission) * Year 2: €75k OTE (Basic, Bonus, Commission plus expenses) * Year 3: €90k OTE (Basic, Bonus, Commission plus expenses) * Year 4 \+ (Senior Financial Planner) €120k. A sign\-on bonus is applicable to candidates with an APA or QFA designation. Inclusion in Company Pension Scheme. Inclusion in Company Life Cover Scheme after year 1\. Extra annual leave day every year for the first five years. Wellness days after 6 months. **Education.** An Education bursary of up to €6,000 is available. This is paid towards qualifying the employee as a Financial Planner (QFA \& CFP exams). \*Please note this role is hybrid\-based, time is split between office \& home. Full\-time attendance in the office is required for the first 4\-5 weeks for training \& induction purposes. \*\*Only applications that are accompanied with a Cover Letter, outlining why you want this specific role, will be considered. *Due to the very high number of applications received for this role, we will only be in contact with candidates whom we are inviting to the first interview stage. Thank you for your understanding.* Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €32,000\.00\-€70,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Additional leave * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Company pension * On\-site parking * Wellness program * Work from home Ability to commute/relocate: * DUBLIN 15, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (preferred) Application question(s): * Do you have a QFA or APA designation? Experience: * sales: 1 year (preferred) Work Location: Hybrid remote in DUBLIN 15, County Dublin (D15\)

title: Junior Technical Analyst
description: **Junior Technical Analyst** The Aviation Advisory team founded in 2016 has since established itself as best in class across its service offerings, growing to a team of 60\+ staff in just 8 years. Current client offerings include; aircraft lease management, data migration management, aircraft maintenance reserve reviews, technical aircraft expertise, deal appraisals for investment decision making, and a broad range of accounting and finance offerings to its clients. The team is currently undertaking lease management for a fleet size of approximately 1,400 leases and provides professional services to 13 out of the Top 15 Aircraft Lessors. **The Opportunity** We have an opportunity for an entry level technical analyst, who is interested in working within the GT Aviation Lease Services team. The successful candidate will be joining a team that has gone through significant growth in recent years with ambitious plans to grow the Aviation Lease Services offering. This is an opportunity for someone with the ability to understand problems quickly to work with the number one provider of Aviation Lease Services to the aircraft leasing industry in Dublin – you will be immersed in the industry with high calibre colleagues. **Roles \& Responsibilities** * Fleet technical records data management, documentation updating and control * Assist with asset review system control and assessments * Manage and administer the technical specification database and reporting * Input monthly utilization and review component status changes * Maintain up to date technical knowledge of each aircraft by type * Input maintain and review fleet’s aircraft specifications, aircraft technical information, equipment data and specifications * Analyzing and updating shop visit events * Assist with MR Claims process **Job Requirements** * Aeronautical Engineering, Aviation Technology Degree or Equivalent * Detail\-Orientated and organized * Meet deadlines and be able to prioritize * Must be diligent, thorough, numerate with a keen attention to detail * Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Excel, Word, Outlook * Previous experience of working in aviation is a distinct advantage

title: Junior Technical Analyst
description: **Job Description** **Junior Technical Analyst** The Aviation Advisory team founded in 2016 has since established itself as best in class across its service offerings, growing to a team of 60\+ staff in just 8 years. Current client offerings include; aircraft lease management, data migration management, aircraft maintenance reserve reviews, technical aircraft expertise, deal appraisals for investment decision making, and a broad range of accounting and finance offerings to its clients. This is an opportunity for someone with the ability to understand problems quickly to work with the number one provider of Aviation Lease Services to the aircraft leasing industry in Dublin – you will be immersed in the industry with high calibre colleagues. **Roles \& Responsibilities** * Fleet technical records data management, documentation updating and control * Assist with asset review system control and assessments * Manage and administer the technical specification database and reporting * Input monthly utilization and review component status changes * Maintain up to date technical knowledge of each aircraft by type * Input maintain and review fleet’s aircraft specifications, aircraft technical information, equipment data and specifications * Analyzing and updating shop visit events * Assist with MR Claims process **Job Requirements** * Aeronautical Engineering, Aviation Technology Degree or Equivalent * Detail\-Orientated and organized * Meet deadlines and be able to prioritize * Must be diligent, thorough, numerate with a keen attention to detail * Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Excel, Word, Outlook * Previous experience of working in aviation is a distinct advantage **About Us** **We are Grant Thornton** Grant Thornton Ireland is rapidly approaching 3,000 people, in 9 offices across Ireland, Isle of Man, Gibraltar and Bermuda. With a presence in over 149 countries around the world and a global network of 73,000 people, we bring our clients the local knowledge, national expertise and global presence to help them succeed – wherever they’re located. At GT, we work as ***trusted advisors*** , bringing local knowledge and national expertise, with a global presence, to help businesses succeed – wherever they are located. We make business more personal by investing in building relationships and empowering our clients to make the right decisions for their organisation now and for the future. Whether that is working with the public sector to build thriving communities, with regulators and financial institutions to build trust, or with a diverse range of businesses to help them achieve their goals, Grant Thornton Ireland work hard to support clients to act on the issues that matter. **At GT Ireland we don’t just predict your future, we build it** . **A Career at GT** Looking for a more fulfilling role in professional services? One where fresh thinking, collaboration and diversity are valued? At Grant Thornton we do things differently. **What does this mean for you?** A career in a more inclusive working environment, a more collaborative work culture, a more supported, flexible working role, more possibilities to grow and more opportunities to help shape the future for your clients. We respect and value your experience. And we want you to bring your authentic self to work and be at your best. It is how it should be. **Grow with us** At Grant Thornton, we care about our people and work hard to make you feel valued. We are all at our best when we are able to be ourselves and we view integrity and authenticity as integral values to bring to our day\-to\-day work\-life at the firm. We are excited to see the personality and perspectives you will bring to our team because we know we will all benefit from them. Diversity of thought, background and experience enables better decision\-making, improves the quality of our delivery, and helps us to meet the needs of our clients. Our firm is built on people and their ideas, so we want to hear all the new perspectives and fresh thinking you have to offer. You form the bedrock of our firm’s best\-practice principles and we will champion you as leaders from day one. **Reward And Benefits** Our reward and benefits are designed to create an environment where our people can flourish. We are committed to building a culture where our people have access to the necessary benefits to help promote a healthy lifestyle and thrive. **Recognition** We want to create a culture of recognition and celebrating success, by saying thank you to people who surpass our expectations and recognising the right values and behaviours. Our Shout Out recognition scheme is our way of highlighting and promoting achievements. Whether you simply want to say thank you, celebrate a special occasion or give an award for doing something exceptional, you can do all of this and more through the scheme.

title: Junior UX/UI designer
description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards. In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other. For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate. **A few examples of your responsibilities** * **Shape high\-leverage product experiences.** Design exceptional UI and UX—from early concepts to polished delivery—using the best available tools to explore more directions, move faster, and raise the quality bar. Ensure every experience is intuitive, effective, and delightful. * **Start with an LLM.** Use AI tools to clarify intent, draft short specs, and surface risks, edge cases, and initial approaches before opening any design tool. Use this work to align quickly with your team and compress the time from ambiguity to direction. * **Prototype and build with AI.** Use Cursor and Claude Code to turn rough concepts into interactive prototypes and generate production\-ready frontend code. You know when to reach for code and when to reach for Figma—and you move fluidly between both to get the best result. * **Co\-create with PMs and engineers from day one.** Define UX requirements early, stay in the build through QA and release, and make engineering\-aware design decisions. * **Validate and measure.** Talk directly with customers, run quick tests, and use what you learn to adjust direction. Track funnels, retention, and adoption to assess design effectiveness and sharpen what ships next. * **Find the highest\-leverage bets from the backlog.** Identify where product improvements can unlock business value, align stakeholders around them, and move fast to deliver. **What we look for** * **Reasoning ability.** Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.** You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.** You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.** You read, write, and speak proficiently in English. **What we offer** * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.** You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.** We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.** Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.** Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work. **Commitment \& contract** Permanent or fixed\-term. Full\-time. **Location** Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries. **The selection process** In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject. If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours. We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times. We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form. **Before you apply** If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again. Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment. To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage. If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

title: Kubernetes Engineer
description: **Overview** We are seeking a Kubernetes Engineer to join our global infrastructure team and help scale, automate, and secure our container orchestration environments across on\-premises and public cloud platforms. As a Kubernetes specialist, you’ll work closely with DevOps, SRE, and security teams to deliver reliable, self\-service, and production\-ready Kubernetes clusters that power mission\-critical applications. **Key Responsibilities** **Cluster Management** * Deploy, manage, and upgrade Kubernetes clusters using tools like kubeadm, EKS, AKS, GKE, or Rancher. * Implement robust RBAC, network policies, ingress controllers, and security frameworks in Kubernetes. **Automation \& IaC** * Automate cluster provisioning and application deployment pipelines using Terraform, Helm, and ArgoCD. * Build reusable modules for consistent infrastructure delivery across staging and production. **CI/CD Integration** * Integrate Kubernetes with modern CI/CD workflows for rapid, safe application delivery. * Support GitOps practices and continuous deployment automation. **Monitoring, Logging \& Troubleshooting** * Implement observability for Kubernetes using Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, and Fluentd/Fluent Bit. * Troubleshoot performance issues, failed pods, memory leaks, and cluster degradation events. **Cloud \& Hybrid Deployments** * Operate Kubernetes workloads across AWS, Azure, GCP, and hybrid/on\-prem environments. * Use tools like Velero, Kasten, or Stash for backup/restore strategies in Kubernetes. **Collaboration \& Support** * Partner with application developers, SREs, and security teams to implement best practices. * Serve as a technical advisor on cloud\-native architectures and containerization s **Experience/Qualification Required** * Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) – Highly preferred * Certified Kubernetes Application Developer (CKAD) * Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist (CKS) * AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional * Google Professional Cloud DevOps Engineer * HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate * Linux Foundation Certified System Administrator (LFCS) **Nice to have:** * Experience with GitOps tools (ArgoCD, Flux). * Observability tools like Prometheus, Grafana, ELK/EFK stack. * Experience with service mesh technologies (Istio, Linkerd). * Open\-source contributions in Kubernetes\-related projects * Spectro cloud, Incus, Cloud 9 etc… **About InterSystems** InterSystems, a creative data technology provider, delivers a unified foundation for next\-generation applications for healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and supply chain customers in more than 80 countries. Our data platforms solve interoperability, speed, and scalability problems for large organizations around the globe to unlock the power of data and allow people to perceive data in imaginative ways. Established in 1978, InterSystems is committed to excellence through its 24×7 support for customers and partners around the world. Privately held and headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, InterSystems has 38 offices in 28 countries worldwide. For more information, please visit **InterSystems.com** .

title: Laboratory Technician
description: **About PSC Biotech** **Who are we?** PSC Biotech is a leading Biotech Consultancy firm founded in 1996, headquartered in Pomona, California, USA, with Global operations in Ireland, India, Singapore, Australia and the US, serving 350 clients in more than 23 countries worldwide. We provide cloud\-based software solutions for Quality Management and Regulatory Inspections, pharmaceuticals contract manufacturing professionals, and metrology services to our clients. **‘Take your Career to a new Level’** PSC Biotech disrupts the conventional consultancy model by aligning our EVP as one of the unique selling point which includes the opportunity to work with the most talented cohort of like\-minded professionals operating in the Pharma/ Biotech Industry. We offer a permanent contract of employment giving exposure of working in Top Pharmaceutical client sites in a diverse\-cultural work setting. **Employee Value Proposition** Employees are the “heartbeat” of PSC Biotech, we provide unparalleled empowering career development though Learning \& Development in\-house training mentorship through constant guidance to facilitate career progression. We believe in creating high performing teams that can exceed our client’s expectations with regards to quality of all scalable and business unit deliverables, staying under budget and ensuring timelines for our deliverables are being met. **Job Purpose:** The Laboratory Technician will support GMP laboratory operations by ensuring that laboratory facilities, equipment, and consumables are maintained in a compliant, safe, and efficient manner. The role is critical in sustaining laboratory readiness through effective cleaning, inventory management, and adherence to health, safety, and environmental requirements. **Requirements** **Key Responsibilities:** **Laboratory Support \& Housekeeping:** * Perform glass washing, drying, and appropriate storage of laboratory glassware in compliance with GMP requirements. * Clean and sterilize laboratory equipment, utensils, and work surfaces to maintain a safe and compliant working environment. * Carry out routine cleaning of the Central Testing Unit (CTU) in accordance with approved procedures and schedules. * Maintain 5S standards for laboratory areas, including fridges and freezers, ensuring organization, cleanliness, and clear labelling at all times. **Inventory \& Materials Management** * Monitor laboratory consumables and inventory levels on a routine basis. * Restock laboratory supplies as required to ensure uninterrupted laboratory operations. * Report inventory shortages, expired materials, or damaged items to senior laboratory staff in a timely manner. * Ensure materials are stored according to defined conditions and GMP requirements. **Health, Safety \& Environmental Compliance:** * Strictly adhere to health and safety regulations, including the correct use of personal protective equipment (PPE). * Dispose of hazardous and non\-hazardous laboratory waste in line with site procedures and regulatory requirements. * Immediately report safety concerns, incidents, or near misses in accordance with procedures. * Support a strong safety\-first culture within the laboratory environment. **GMP \& Quality Compliance:** * Perform all assigned duties in compliance with cGMP, company policies, and site procedures. * Accurately complete required documentation and records in line with data integrity principles. * Support audit and inspection readiness by maintaining laboratory areas at required standards. **Communication \& Teamwork:** * Communicate effectively with laboratory colleagues and senior staff regarding laboratory status, inventory issues, and safety concerns. * Work collaboratively as part of a GMP laboratory team to support daily operations and site objectives. **Key Skills \& Competencies:** * Attention to Detail: Ability to consistently maintain high standards of cleanliness, organization, and compliance. * Safety Compliance: Strong awareness of laboratory safety, chemical handling, and waste disposal requirements. * Organization: Ability to manage multiple tasks while maintaining orderly laboratory environments. * Communication: Clear and effective communication when reporting issues, shortages, or safety concerns. * Reliability: Demonstrates a dependable and responsible approach to routine laboratory duties. * Teamwork: Works well with others to support laboratory and site goals. **Qualifications \& Experience (Desirable):** * Previous experience working in a laboratory or GMP\-regulated environment is an advantage. * Familiarity with laboratory housekeeping, inventory management, or regulated cleaning activities is beneficial. * Training in health and safety or laboratory practices is desirable; full training will be provided.

title: Legal Counsel - Corporate
description: At Perrigo, we are driven by our mission to ***Makes Lives Better Through Trusted Health and Wellness Solutions, Accessible to All***. We are proud to be a Top 10 player in the European Consumer Self\-Care market and the largest U.S. store brand provider of over the counter and infant formula. Dedicated to providing ***The Best Self\-Care for Everyone***, we are the people behind the brands you trust. We are Opill®, Compeed®, Solpadeine®, NiQuitin®, and many more. **We Are Perrigo**. We are committed to enhancing the wellbeing of our colleagues and consumers alike. We pride ourselves on fostering an inclusive, collaborative culture where each person can experience a sense of belonging. Join us on our One Perrigo journey as we evolve to win in self\-care. **Description Overview** ------------------------ This is an exciting opportunity to apply your legal expertise to Perrigo’s Corporate Legal team, which operates at the intersection of law and business and provides strategic legal counsel across areas such as Business Development, Corporate Governance, Corporate Finance, Tax, Treasury, and Investor Relations. As **Legal Counsel – Corporate**, you will play a crucial role in supporting the Deputy General Counsel across a broad range of high‑impact corporate matters, with **a primary focus on leading and supporting M\&A and strategic transactions**, corporate governance for a **public company headquartered in Ireland**, and complex global corporate operations. The selected candidate will be based at our Global Headquarters in **Dublin, Ireland**. While Dublin is strongly preferred, we will also consider exceptional candidates for our **London, England** office. A hybrid working schedule applies in either location. **Scope of the Role** --------------------- * M\&A: Lead and support Perrigo’s global M\&A and strategic transaction activity, including: + Managing and coordinating cross‑functional legal workstreams on acquisitions, divestitures, strategic partnerships, and other transformative transactions. + Conducting and overseeing due diligence; drafting, reviewing, and negotiating transaction agreements; and supporting carve‑out, integration planning, restructuring, and related corporate approvals. + Preparing and coordinating antitrust/competition and other regulatory filings across multiple jurisdictions. + Advising senior leadership on transaction risks, opportunities, and structuring considerations in a dynamic multinational environment. * Board \& Corporate Governance: Together with a Legal Analyst, play a critical role in supporting Perrigo’s Board of Directors and governance framework for a NYSE‑listed, Irish‑domiciled company, including: + Preparing and managing materials for quarterly Board and Committee meetings, including agendas, resolutions, briefing memos, and minutes. + Partnering with the Deputy General Counsel to maintain, update, and enhance global governance policies and governance best practices across U.S. and Irish legal frameworks. + Supporting the Annual General Meeting (AGM), including planning, drafting AGM materials, managing shareholder and regulatory requirements in Ireland, and ensuring compliance with Irish and U.S. governance norms. + Leading quarterly governance updates, monitoring regulatory and stewardship‑related developments (e.g. ISS, Glass Lewis, Irish law, NYSE), and presenting trends to senior leadership. + Developing and delivering training modules and education materials for Directors, including Ireland\-specific governance dynamics and cross‑jurisdictional fiduciary duties. * Subsidiary Entity Management: Together with the Corporate Governance Analyst, serve as a senior legal advisor on Perrigo’s global corporate matters, including: + Overseeing global entity management, board/corporate secretarial functions, and corporate recordkeeping across Europe, North America, and other regions. + Supporting subsidiary governance enhancements and internal corporate simplification efforts. * Corporate Finance, Disclosure and Securities Regulation: Provide targeted, strategic support on U.S. securities law matters, including: + Assisting with periodic reporting and disclosure considerations. + Reviewing press releases, earnings materials, and investor communications for legal and risk considerations. + Advising on insider trading, Section 16, and corporate disclosure controls as needed. * Tax \& Treasury: Provide targeted, strategic support on tax \& treasury matters, including financing activities; M\&A\-related tax structuring; and domestic and international tax matters. * General Corporate Matters: Provide legal support on a broad range of general corporate matters, including entity management, corporate compliance, and other day\-to\-day corporate legal issues impacting the company’s operations. Serve as a cross functional strategic partner to Finance, Tax, and Global Business Development. **Experience Required** ----------------------- * A qualified and licensed attorney; admission to practice law in Ireland or England. * Typically 6\-10\+ years of legal experience working with international publicly traded companies, preferably with a combination of law firm and in\-house experience. * The ideal candidate will have a proven track record of law firm M\&A experience and adjacent corporate practice areas. * Demonstrated ability to manage senior executive relations and ability to collaborate and build relations with stakeholders across functional and organizational lines. * Strong understanding of relevant laws and regulations governing the industry, combined with an ability to explain complex legal concepts in simple, actionable terms to stakeholders located in different jurisdictions. * Strong strategic thinking and business acumen, with the ability to balance legal considerations with business objectives. * Exceptional leadership, interpersonal and communication skills, including well\-developed interpersonal skills and strong written communication skills. * Discretion \& Integrity – the ability to handle sensitive information with the highest level of confidentiality and ethics. * Project management skills – the ability to manage multiple legal matters and deadlines. Familiarity with legal tech tools is also a bonus. **Benefits** We believe our people are our greatest asset. Alongside competitive compensation, we offer benefits tailored to supporting you and your family, as well as career development opportunities to ensure you feel valued and supported, both professionally and personally. Find out more about **Total Rewards** at Perrigo. **Hybrid Working Approach** We love our offices and the setting they provide for in\-person collaboration and celebration. But we also appreciate the opportunity to work remotely can energise you too, so we promote flexibility with the ability to work two days a week from home in many roles. We are proud to be included in the Forbes list of "America's Best Employers by State 2024". Find out more **here** **Applicants please note:** To apply to this position please click the APPLY button at the bottom of the application. (The SAVE button will only save your profile information but not submit an application for this open position.) Thank you. *We are an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or other characteristics protected by law. \#weareperrigo*

title: Legal Counsel – Multinational Tech Company
description: My client is looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to join their team in Dublin. They are a major Multinational company growing their team in Ireland at scale. You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin, Ireland. Hybrid.** Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. title: Marketing Administrator

title: Marketing Graduate
description: **About the role:** The Marketing Graduate is an entry\-level role within the Marketing team, designed to provide hands\-on experience across a broad range of marketing disciplines including campaigns, content, digital, partnerships, and events. Reporting to the Residential Marketing Manager, the role supports the delivery of both B2B and residential marketing activity, contributing to live campaigns, activations, and day\-to\-day marketing operations. This is a structured graduate programme offering exposure to a fast\-paced commercial environment, with opportunities to develop practical skills and gain end\-to\-end experience across the marketing function. **Please note, this is a 12\-month contract.** **Key Responsibilities:** Campaign \& Project Support * Support the delivery of marketing campaigns across residential and B2B audiences * Assist in the coordination of campaign activity from planning through to execution * Support the preparation of campaign materials, presentations and internal updates Partnerships \& Events Support * Support the delivery of partnerships, sponsorships and marketing activations * Assist with the coordination of events, including logistics and on\-the\-day support * Key role in the execution of hero partnership deliverables * Help manage marketing materials and collateral for activations Marketing Operations \& Administration * Support the onboarding of suppliers, including setting up new supplier forms and documentation * Assist with preparing and submitting materials for regulatory and legal approval * Support the creation and distribution of customer sustainability certificates, ensuring accuracy and timely delivery to business customers * Create and maintain marketing reports, including press coverage and campaign summaries * Support the creation of presentations and slides for internal and external use * Assist with budget tracking and general marketing administration * Support the coordination of internal marketing team events, including offsites \& engagement initiatives * Bring ideas to support team culture, collaboration and team engagement * Manage event logistics, coordination and budget tracking with support from the Marketing Director Content \& Social Support * Assist with the creation of content for social media, website and marketing materials * Support the planning and scheduling of social media content * Work with the Content \& Copy Specialist and wider team to ensure content is aligned to brand Digital \& Website Support * Assist with updating website content and supporting digital marketing activity * Support campaign setup tasks and coordination with digital partners where required * Help ensure content is accurate and up to date across digital touchpoints Research \& Insights * Support the gathering of market, competitor and customer insights * Assist in compiling campaign performance reports and summaries * Help identify trends and opportunities to inform marketing activity **What you will gain:** * Exposure to a wide range of marketing disciplines * Hands\-on experience working on live campaigns and projects * Opportunity to work across both B2B and consumer marketing * Development of practical skills across content, campaigns, partnerships and digital * Insight into working within a commercial, fast\-paced organisation **Candidate Profile:** We are seeking a recent graduate in Marketing, Business, or a related discipline with a strong interest in building a career in marketing, digital, and communications. The ideal candidate will be highly organised, proactive, and eager to learn, with strong written and verbal communication skills and the ability to work effectively as part of a team. They will demonstrate a positive, hands\-on attitude and a willingness to take initiative across a variety of tasks, from campaign support and content creation to events, digital updates, and reporting. Strong attention to detail, time management skills, and an interest in working in a fast\-paced, commercial environment are essential. The successful candidate will be curious, collaborative, and motivated to develop a broad skill set across multiple areas of marketing. **About Flogas:** Part of the DCC Group, Flogas Ireland was established in 1978 supplying Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG) throughout the island and to this day remains one of the leading suppliers in Ireland, servicing a diverse range of market sectors, including commercial, agricultural, and domestic. Over the years, Flogas has evolved and grown as an Energy business, and with the acquisition of Budget Energy, based in Derry, and Flogas Enterprise Solutions, we are now also a leading supplier of Natural Gas and Electricity, as well as Renewables, including Corporate Power Purchase Agreements, and energy services in both residential and commercial markets across the island of Ireland. Flogas actively supports micro\-generation, which involves generating electricity from renewable sources such as solar photovoltaic (PV), micro\-wind, micro\-hydro, and micro\-renewable combined heat and power We have an esteemed reputation within the Energy Industry and have been awarded the NSAI Quality System Certificate from the I.S. EN ISO 9001 Series. As a Flogas Group, we are now driving our growth strategy to achieve our vision of being Irelands leading provider of total energy solutions, meeting our customers changing needs and delivering to the highest possible standard. **Why work with us?** Flogas is a well\-established business within the DCC group and is continuing to grow, becoming a top provider in the energy sector, making it a very exciting time to be part of the Flogas Group. We offer a warm and welcoming environment, valuing our people and their input into the business. We encourage a collaborative workplace, where everyone feels included and heard. **Benefits:** * Competitive Salary * Defined Pension Contribution * Healthcare * Paid maternity/paternity leave * Staff discounts * Hybrid work options (role dependant) * Health and Wellbeing Supports * Learning and Development * Career Advancement is important to us in Flogas. All our roles are advertised internally, and we encourage internal progression right across the business. Flogas is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

title: Mathematics Specialist (Masters/PhDs)
description: **Mathematics Expert (AI Training)** **About The Role** What if your deep knowledge of mathematics could directly shape how AI reasons, solves problems, and communicates with millions of people around the world? We're looking for Mathematics Experts — Masters and PhD holders — to help build and refine the mathematical reasoning capabilities of cutting\-edge AI models. This is a fully remote, flexible contract role. You'll work asynchronously alongside world\-class AI research teams, applying your expertise to problems that matter. No prior AI experience needed — just serious mathematical depth and a sharp eye for rigor. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Develop, solve, and review advanced mathematical problems with real\-world relevance and complexity * Design rigorous problem statements across algebra, calculus, statistics, discrete mathematics, or related fields * Evaluate AI\-generated mathematical reasoning for correctness, depth, and clarity * Collaborate asynchronously with AI researchers and domain experts to improve model performance * Ensure mathematical rigor, precision, and intellectual depth across all deliverables * Work independently on task\-based assignments — fully on your own schedule **Who You Are** * Holds a Master's or PhD in Mathematics or a closely related field * Deep expertise in one or more of: algebra, calculus, statistics, or discrete mathematics * Comfortable coding in Python, MATLAB, or Mathematica for research or applied problem\-solving * Exceptionally detail\-oriented with strong analytical and written communication skills * Fluent in English with the ability to articulate complex mathematical concepts clearly * Self\-motivated and reliable when working independently without supervision **Nice to Have** * Prior experience with data annotation, data quality evaluation, or AI training workflows * Background in applied mathematics, mathematical modelling, or computational research * Experience writing or reviewing technical content for academic or professional audiences * Familiarity with AI tools or language model evaluation as an end user **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects alongside leading research labs * Fully remote and flexible — work when and where it suits you * Freelance autonomy with the structure of meaningful, intellectually stimulating work * Make a direct, tangible impact on how AI understands and reasons through mathematics * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch
